name: The Midlife Crisis | Online
description: The idea of a "midlife crisis" has been around for decades. But is that really what most people experience?Join us for an open conversation about the realities of midlife: what changes, what surprises us, and what this phase of life can make possible.We often hear midlife described as a crisis. Yet for many people, it feels more like a transition. A period of questioning, recalibration, and new possibilities.Work may feel different. Relationships evolve. Priorities shift. What once seemed important may no longer hold the same meaning, while new interests, aspirations, and ways of living begin to emerge.This 90-minute online sharing circle offers a space to reflect on these changes, hear diverse perspectives, and connect with others navigating their own midlife journey. Whether you're rethinking work, relationships, identity, purpose, or what comes next, this conversation invites curiosity rather than certainty.Come as you are. Leave with new perspectives, fresh questions, and a sense that you are not navigating this transition alone.

name: Asymmetry in Play: Indian Hunt Games
description: Gallery XXL and Jildsaaz invite you to an after hours board game session that explores the family of traditional hunt games commonly known as Goats and Tigers, one of the oldest surviving strategy game traditions of South Asia. Known by different names across regions, including Aadu Puli Aatam (Tamil Nadu), Bagh Chal (Nepal and North India), Sher Bakri (Punjab), Bagha Chheli (Odisha), Bagh Bandi (West Bengal), and Vagh Aur Bakri (Gujarat), the game belongs to a category of asymmetric strategy games in which unequal forces are pitted against one another. Played without dice or any element of chance, the game relies entirely on strategy. One player controls a small number of predators while the other commands a larger group of prey animals. Despite their numerical disadvantage, the predators possess greater mobility and power, creating a dynamic contest that mirrors real-world relationships between strength and collective action.Historically, the game was widely played by ordinary communities due to its simple material requirements: a geometric grid scratched onto the ground and a set of distinguishable counters. Archaeological and architectural evidence of these boards survives in temple courtyards, cave complexes, and public spaces across India, including sites such as the Khajuraho Temple complex, Shravanabelagola, the Chamundeshwari Temple in Mysuru, and the Veerabhadra Temple at Lepakshi.Participants are divided into small groups and introduced to the rules and strategies of the game.Format: 2 Players per boardRounds: Multiple rounds with player rotationDate : Friday, June 26, 2026Time : 7 - 9 PMVenue : Gallery XXLTicket price : INR 1250/- including taxes

name: The Procrastireaders
description: The Procrastireaders: Love is Love - Pride Month ReadingOverviewA slow reading circle designed around comfort, not pressure.People come together to read, exchange thoughts, and return to books in a way that feels lighter and more approachable. Stories, shared slowly.This June, Procrastireaders is reading stories about queer love. The joy, the tenderness, the ordinary and extraordinary ways people find each other. Stories where being queer is not the conflict to be resolved but simply part of who the characters are. If that sounds like your kind of read, come join us.What is Stories of Hope?Stories of Hope is a community built around a simple idea, that people need places where they can slow down and be heard. We create small, intentional spaces for writing, listening, creating, and reflecting. There is no expectation to perform and no pressure to have the right words. Just a space to pause and be with what is already there.What to ExpectDiscussion First: We open with a conversation around the theme, the books you brought, and what drew you to them. No right answers, just an honest exchange.Silent Reading Block: We close with dedicated quiet time to actually sit with your book. Good lighting, AC, and fellow readers for company.Bring Your Own Book: Carry whatever you are currently reading or something from your "to be read" pile that needs your attention.Details🗓️ Date: 27th June (Saturday)⏰ Time: 11:30 AM📍 Location: Unlocked, 32nd Avenue🎟️ Fee: ₹700

name: Mughal Princess’s World Through Food
description: Dastarkhwan-e-BegamaatZaikanama presents two lunches at the dastarkhwan of the Mughal BegamaatTwo women, a century apart, recovered through what they ate.The official record kept the wars and lost the women. Gulbadan wrote the household down herself because no one else would. Jahanara ran a court and a city and left her mark on Delhi’s streets. This is their world, rebuilt from the period’s own logic and read through the cloth they gathered around. No table. A dastarkhwan, the spread laid on the ground that a meal sits on and a household forms around.Six courses each lunch, served family-style.Gulbadan. The early Mughal world, still in motion. A dastarkhwan rolled up at dawn and unrolled by the next river. Exile, return, Kabul remembered across three kingdoms, a palate carrying Central Asia in a fold of fabric and slowly learning Hindustan.Jahanara. The same cloth, a hundred years on, spread across marble. The empire has stopped moving. Rosewater, saffron, the confidence of a court at its height, and a princess who shaped the city it ruled from.One lunch leaves home. The other arrives. Together they trace a single line, from the tent to the marble, from longing to power.The dishes stay between us and the guests who sit down to them. Seats are limited.🗓️ 27–28 June 2026🕜 1:30 PM onwards📍 South Delhi

name: #lecture33: How to Create Trustworthy DPI
description: #lecture33After a short summer break, we're excited to be back with another Pint of View lecture and we're returning with a question that quietly shapes all of our digital lives.Every day, most of us unlock our phones, make a UPI payment, verify our identity with Aadhaar, or access digital public services without giving it a second thought. These systems have become so seamless that we rarely stop to ask a simple question:The real question isn't whether these systems work most of the time. It's whether we can be confident they'll behave correctly when it matters most. Can that confidence come from testing alone, or is there a way to prove it mathematically?As India continues to build one of the world's most ambitious Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) ecosystems, trust is no longer just a policy question it's a technical one. A single flaw in software handling identity, financial transactions, or personal data can have consequences at an unprecedented scale.In this session, Dr. Vaishnavi Sundararajan, Assistant Professor at IIT Delhi, will introduce us to the fascinating world of formal verification, a branch of computer science that uses mathematics and logic to prove that software systems are secure, privacy-preserving, and function exactly as intended.Together, we'll explore questions that don't usually make it into everyday conversations about technology. Is strong encryption enough? Can software really be proven to be secure? How do governments know that systems handling billions of transactions won't fail in unexpected ways? And as India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act begins to shape how personal data is governed, what role can mathematical proofs play in building digital systems people can genuinely trust?Whether you're interested in technology, public policy, cybersecurity, mathematics, or simply curious about the invisible systems that power everyday life, this conversation offers an opportunity to look beneath the surface of the digital world we rely on every day.Event detailsSaturday, 27 June 20264:00 PM – 6:00 PMThe Pint Room, Vasant Vihar, New DelhiJoin us for an evening of thoughtful conversation, fresh perspectives, and the kind of questions that stay with you long after the lecture ends.

name: 4D Polymers: 3 Dimensions & Beyond
description: Join us for a lecture that explores a future where materials don't simply exist, they respond, transform, heal, and even generate their own energy.About the LectureMost materials are passive.A bandage covers a wound. An implant supports damaged tissue. A scaffold holds cells in place.But what if materials could do more?What if they could change shape over time, respond to their environment, generate electrical signals, and actively participate in the healing process?This lecture introduces the emerging world of 4D biomaterials — materials designed not only in three dimensions, but with a fourth dimension: time.Drawing from cutting-edge research in regenerative medicine and bioengineering, the session explores how scientists are creating materials that can mimic some of the remarkable properties of living tissue. These next-generation biomaterials are capable of programmed transformations, self-powered stimulation, and dynamic interactions with the cells around them.The lecture explores:What 4D printing is and how it differs from conventional 3D printingHow materials can be designed to change shape and function over timeThe role of bioelectric signals in tissue healing and regenerationHow self-powered biomaterials can generate electrical stimulation without batteries or external energy sourcesWhy these technologies could transform the future of healthcare, implants, and regenerative medicineAlong the way, the session offers a glimpse into a future where materials are no longer passive tools but active participants in biological processes.About the SpeakerDr. Amit Nain is an Assistant Professor at the Kusuma School of Biological Sciences, IIT Delhi, where he leads research focused on developing next-generation biomaterials for tissue regeneration and advanced healthcare applications.His laboratory works at the intersection of materials science, biology, and engineering, designing bioinstructive materials that can sense, respond, and support healing processes within the body.Dr. Nain completed his B.Tech. in Biotechnology from VIT University and earned his PhD in Nano Science and Technology from National Taiwan University, where his research focused on antimicrobial nanomaterials.His work has been recognised internationally, including being named a 2024 Emerging Investigator by the Royal Society of Chemistry and a 2025 Rising Star by the American Chemical Society. He has published over 35 research articles, book chapters, and conference proceedings, and currently serves on the Early Career Board of Biomacromolecules.Outside the lab, he is a Bollywood enthusiast, poetry lover, road-trip addict, and an unapologetic fan of Friends, Breaking Bad, Homeland, and Money Heist.Your ticket includes a 200/- voucher for F&B About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: Haunted Walk: Mehrauli Archaeological Park
description: Join us for a hauntingly beautiful night walk through the Mehrauli Archaeological Park, a place where centuries of Delhi’s history come alive—alongside tales of the unseen. In this special segment, Ruhaani Raatein, we delve into the world of djinns, the mystical beings believed to reside in this ancient land.Guided by a historian and storyteller, we’ll explore how djinns are viewed and worshipped in Islamic traditions, and how folklore continues to shape the spiritual character of Mehrauli even today.Our walk will take you through: The tomb of Jamali Kamali, often called the Gay Taj Mahal for its poetic mystique and timeless love story A haunting 500-year-old baoli (stepwell)The remnants of a British-era swimming pool, hidden in plain sight A peaceful lakeside café, perfect for ending the evening with a quiet reflection or a warm chaiAs night falls and shadows grow, let the stories guide you through one of Delhi’s oldest, most magical spaces—where the past is never truly past.

name: The Hidden History Of Queer Lives
description: Join us for a lecture that challenges one of the most persistent myths about queerness in India: that it is new.About the LectureHistory, as it is usually told, rarely makes room for queer lives.Queer people appear as footnotes, court cases, scandals, anthropological curiosities, or cautionary tales. Entire worlds of belonging, intimacy, resistance, and community are left out of the official record.But they were always here.This lecture traces a longer and richer history of queerness in India—one that stretches far beyond contemporary debates about identity, rights, and representation.Moving through colonial archives, legal history, community formations, and political movements, the session explores:What existed before Section 377—and what colonial rule sought to eraseHow communities such as hijras, kothis, aravanis, and jogatis built their own social worldsThe long struggle for queer rights in IndiaWhy remembering queer histories changes how we understand the presentAnchored by the anthology Queer India Now, this lecture is ultimately about memory itself: who gets remembered, who gets forgotten, and what becomes possible when lost histories are recovered.About the SpeakersDhamini Ratnam is National Culture Editor at Hindustan Times and an award-winning journalist specialising in gender and sexuality. Over a seventeen-year career spanning publications such as Mint, Mumbai Mirror, and Mid-Day, she has written extensively about LGBTQIA+ lives in India and broader questions of social change.Dhrubo Jyoti is a Delhi-based writer and editor whose work focuses on questions of caste, queerness, and representation. Their work seeks to foreground voices and histories that have often remained at the margins of mainstream LGBTQIA+ narratives.Your ticket includes a 250/- coupon redemable on F&BAbout unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from St. Stephen’s, Delhi who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. Each event is an invitation to spend an evening with an idea.

name: Night Heritage walk :  Humayun's Tomb & Museum
description: Step into the grandeur of Humayun's Tomb with us for an immersive Night heritage walk that goes beyond stone and symmetry.Curated by Darwesh Heritage Walks in collaboration with Sabhyata Foundation.We will not only explore the monument’s magnificent architecture, its Persian influences, and its place in the evolution of Mughal design — but also rediscover Humayun himself, perhaps the most underestimated of the Mughals. His struggles, exile, return, and legacy shaped the empire in ways we rarely pause to notice."Driven from his throne, yet never from his destiny's embrace,Humayun's return laid the path on which Akbar would leave his trace"Our journey will then continue to the Humayun's Tomb Museum — because no visit to the tomb is truly complete without experiencing the stories, artefacts, and narratives preserved there.Come, walk through history — and meet the emperor behind the monument.

name: From mehfils to movies: Courtesans of India
description: A Heritage Baithak at Qudsia BaghWhat happens when history remembers a woman only through the lens of morality and forgets her art, intellect, and influence?Join us for a fascinating journey into the world of India's courtesans—Nagarvadhus, Devadasis, Kanchanis, Tawaifs, and Nautch Girls—women who were once among the most accomplished artists, poets, musicians, patrons, and cultural icons of their age. Many enjoyed wealth, education, and social influence, supported temples and religious institutions, shaped artistic traditions, and moved comfortably within royal courts.Yet, with changing political and colonial attitudes, these women found themselves increasingly stigmatized, their identities reduced to stereotypes and their contributions pushed to the margins of history. We will trace this remarkable transformation and explore how many eventually carried their artistic legacy into the emerging world of Indian cinema, becoming singers, actors, dancers, and pioneers of the film industry.Through stories of power, performance, patronage, and prejudice, this walk seeks to rediscover the women who helped shape India's cultural landscape but whose voices have often been forgotten.Join Darwesh Heritage Walks at Qudsia Bagh as we uncover the extraordinary lives of India's courtesans—the custodians of art, culture, and memory.

name: QueerProv: Come As You Are
description: Gallery XXL invites you to QueerProv: Come As You Are for an improvisation jam. QueerProv is an all queer, transgender and women inclusive space for improv jams and performances. Focused on dismantling the existing hetero-patriarchal structures, it's a space to explore and build queer storytelling through play, spontaneity, humour and interactive activities.Open to queer, trans and women folxAbout Shruti Garg Therapist by day, stand-up comedian by night. Shruti Garg (she/they) is a drama and movement therapist, queer-affirmative mental health professional, faculty at VIMHANS, and founder of QueerProv India. Trained at the Royal Central School of Speech and Drama (UK), she works at the intersection of mental health, humour, theatre, and inclusion.Date : Sunday, June 28, 2026Time : 11AM - 1 PMVenue : Gallery XXLTicket price : INR 550/- including taxesFAQsQ. What is the duration?A. 2 hoursQ. Who is this workshop for?A. People who are exploring or identify themselves on the LGBTQIA++ spectrum. Assigned female at birth or those who identify themselves as women⁠⁠Q. Do I need to prep something before the workshop?A. Wear comfortable clothing so that you can engage freely in physical activities and improvisationsQ. Do I need any prior experience in theatre or improv theatre to attend?A. You don’t need to have any prior experience in theatre or improv, your interest in performance and play is a plus though!For any further information, contact us at info@galleryxxl.com or +91 93248 57354

name: Exaaj- An Assamese Lunch
description: Exaaj khabole ahibo. Come have a meal with us. On 28th June, we’re bringing Assam to Sidecar.In Assamese, Exaaj means a meal. Not just food on a plate, but the act of gathering around a table, sharing stories, and being fed with care. For one afternoon, The Nourished Ritual brings that spirit to Delhi with a menu inspired by Bohag Bihu and the flavours of an Assamese home kitchen.Expect river fish, bamboo shoot, fiddlehead fern, pitikas, dal tenga and dishes that rarely make it onto restaurant menus, cooked the way they’ve always been cooked.No tasting menu. No fine dining theatrics. Just a proper Assamese meal shared around the table.₹2,800 veg thaali (AI) · ₹3,500 non-veg thaali (AI)(Includes set thaali and two cocktails)

Toontooni's Table is bringing Lallan Sweets to life, course by course.Join us for an intimate 6-course lunch inspired by Srishti Chaudhary's beloved novel, followed by a meet & greet with the author herself. Every dish on the table tells a story. Come hungry, leave with a signed Collector's Edition.

name: FLOW STATE
description: A Range Artists’ Intervention Date : Sunday, June 28, 2026Time : 3PM - 8PMVenue : Gallery XXLEarly bird ticket price : INR 1000/- including taxesLast minute tickets : INR 1250/- including taxes"It was like floating. I was carried on by the flow."— Mihaly Csikszentmihályi, Flow (1990)What happens when the process becomes the artwork?Gallery XXL and Range Artists Collective invites you into an evening that resists the finished art object and instead inquires into one that unfolds, accumulates, and dissolves in real time. Flow is their first intervention: a response to the ongoing exhibition Paper and Play, where visitors step out of the role of audience and into the work itself.Rooted in psychologist Mihaly Csikszentmihályi's concept of the flow state, the rare condition of complete immersion where action and consciousness melt together, the evening is structured around participation, presence, and process.Segment I: Process as ArtworkOpen Studio Interventions by:Live Painting & Collective Mark-Making — ShreyshtPaper Mosaics & Collage — DevnaWax, Ink & Material Experiments — SaloniTypography & Spatial Interventions — PranjalSculpture, Fashion & Identity — FarisMovement Activation — Priyanka SarkarSegment II: Paper to SystemPerformances and Generative Environments by:Generative Media & Projection Mapping — ParamExperimental Film & Glitch Dreamscapes — AdvaitaCinema as Process: Film Performance — Priyanka Sarkar with beatnykLive Coding & Algorithmic Sound — beatnykImmersive Sonic Landscapes — ImerohiMovement Performance — Mishka GargArtist Bios:Priyanka aka ANKA works across film, movement, sound, and performance. Her practice approaches cinema as a living process where documentation, memory, improvisation, and embodiment continually intersect. @priyankaaasarkarMishka Garg is a movement artist, performer, choreographer and facilitator. Her mediums include movement, photography, films and visuals in the service of aesthetic storytelling, experimentation and higher embodied freedom. @mishkagargShreysht works through live painting and collective image-making, using participation and accumulation as tools for creating evolving visual environments. @shreyshtwadhwaPranjal operates across postmodern typography, graphic design, and curatorial practice, investigating how ideas translate across mediums, disciplines, and communities. @pranjpgSaloni works through material experimentation, embracing imperfection, error, and transformation as methodologies for play and discovery. @saloniimahajanFaris works across fashion, drag, performance, and visual storytelling, exploring questions of identity, belonging, transformation, and self-authorship. @phares__wheelParam is an interdisciplinary artist working with photography, generative systems, projection mapping, and computational media to examine relationships between technology, perception, and image-making. @parmejaaanAdvaita creates experimental films and digital dreamscapes where manipulated imagery, glitch aesthetics, speculative narratives, and surreal visual worlds collide. @sikotropiixImerohi works across electronic music, sound design, and live performance, creating immersive sonic environments that explore atmosphere, memory, and perception. @imerohiibeatnyk explores computational creativity through live coding, algorithmic music, and audiovisual systems, treating code as both instrument and artistic medium. @beatnykDevna works through collage, painting, paper mosaics, and body-based interventions, exploring home, memory, belonging, and process-led forms of making. @de_vigeon

name: Notes From The Modern Invention of Sexuality
description: Join us for a lecture that asks an unsettling question:What if modern society did not become obsessed with sex because it was finally liberated—but because it found new ways to make us confess?About the LectureWe tend to think of talking about sex as an act of freedom. The more openly we speak about our desires, identities, fantasies, and intimacies, the more liberated we become.Michel Foucault was deeply suspicious of this story.In The History of Sexuality, he makes a strange claim: modern societies did not develop through the repression of sex, but through an ever-expanding demand that people speak about it. What began as a religious obligation to confess gradually spread into schools, hospitals, psychiatric institutions, government bureaucracies, and eventually into everyday life itself.The result was not simply greater knowledge about sexuality. It was the creation of sexuality as a category through which people could be classified, monitored, governed, and understood.Drawing on Foucault's account of confession, power, and biopolitics, this lecture explores:How sexuality became a modern object of knowledgeWhy institutions are invested in making intimate life legibleWhat confession has to do with governance and social controlHow reality television, social media, dating apps, and digital platforms transform self-disclosure into a cultural normWhy every search, swipe, message, and online interaction increasingly resembles a confession to a machineAt a moment when we are constantly encouraged to "be ourselves," "share our truth," and "tell our story," this lecture asks a provocative question: when we finally believe we are expressing our most authentic selves, who is listening—and what are they doing with what we reveal?About the SpeakerDr. Ananya Dasgupta is a scholar of literature, film, philosophy, and psychoanalysis. She studied English Literature at Jadavpur University before completing her doctoral research through IIT Bombay and Monash University, Melbourne.Her work explores sexuality and publicness, feminist engagements with law, and the relationship between politics and affect in contemporary culture. Her doctoral research examined figures of extra-legality in Hindi cinema. Her public writing has appeared in Scroll, ASAP Art, and The Ladies Finger.She currently teaches at Ashoka University, where she continues to explore questions of desire, power, selfhood, and the strange ways institutions teach us to speak about ourselves.Your ticket includes a 250/- coupon redeemable on F&B at the venue. About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from St. Stephen’s, Delhi who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. Each event is an invitation to spend an evening with an idea.

name: Among Behemoths: On Tigers, Territory, And People
description: Join us for a lecture that challenges one of the most enduring myths of the natural world:That tigers belong somewhere far away from us.About the LectureWhen we think of tigers, we often imagine a solitary predator lurking deep inside untouched forests — a powerful but distant creature whose world exists entirely apart from our own.Much of modern conservation has been built around this image.But the reality is far more complicated.Across India, tigers move through landscapes shaped not only by forests, but by roads, farms, villages, canals, and human lives. Their stories are intertwined with ours in ways that are often overlooked, revealing a world where conflict and coexistence exist side by side.Drawing on years of fieldwork and a lifetime spent exploring India's forests, this lecture looks beyond the mythology of the tiger to understand the animal itself — its behaviour, its adaptability, and its relationship with the people who share its territory.The session explores:Why many popular assumptions about tigers are misleadingHow tiger behaviour differs from the image of the relentless apex predatorThe changing meaning of "wild" in an increasingly human-shaped worldHow communities and tigers negotiate shared landscapesWhat conservation looks like beyond the idea of keeping nature separate from peopleMoving between ecology, anthropology, conservation, and storytelling, the lecture examines what happens when we stop thinking of wilderness as a place apart and begin seeing it as something deeply entangled with human life.At its core, this is a story about adaptation — not only of tigers, but of all of us.About the SpeakerRudraksha Rishi Mitra is a social innovator, researcher, and founder of Canopy Wild, an immersive wildlife travel and education company dedicated to deepening public engagement with conservation and the natural world.A graduate of the London School of Economics in International Social and Public Policy, he has worked extensively across public policy, sustainable development, and social entrepreneurship. In 2022, he was selected as an Unleash Global Talent, recognising him among the world's leading young social innovators.His research has focused on conservation policy and human–tiger interactions in the Terai Arc Landscape, combining ethnographic methods with ecological inquiry. Over the years, he has explored and documented tiger landscapes across India, including the Nilgiri Biosphere, Annamalais, Terai Arc, and the Dooars.Having spent much of his life among forests and wildlife habitats, he is particularly interested in the ways people and large carnivores continue to share space in a rapidly changing world.Ticket IncludesYour ticket includes a 500/- coupon for F&BAbout unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: National Museum (Indus Valley Civilisation)
description: Step into the fascinating world of the Indus Valley Civilization at the iconic National Museum and uncover the mysteries of one of the world’s oldest urban cultures. From advanced city planning and drainage systems to intricate seals, jewellery, pottery, and trade networks, this walk takes you deep into the lives of a civilization that flourished over 4,500 years ago.Explore the stories hidden behind ancient artefacts, decode symbols that still remain unsolved, and discover how the people of Harappa and Mohenjo-Daro were far ahead of their time. Whether you’re a history enthusiast, a curious explorer, or someone who simply loves untold stories, this immersive museum experience promises a journey through India’s remarkable ancient past.Join us as history comes alive — one artefact, one mystery, and one story at a time.

location.address: Janpath Rd, Central Secretariat, Kartavya Path, New Delhi, Delhi 110011, India

name: Mode of transport - Ate glasshouse
description: Mode of Transport is the final stop of CTRLlab's Summer Coffee Crawl—an evening dedicated to journeys, movement, and the stories that connect people, places, and coffee across distances.As the series concludes, we turn our attention to the paths we travel: the routes coffee takes from farm to cup, the roads that lead us to new experiences, and the moments of movement that quietly shape our lives.Coffee Anchor follows coffee's remarkable journey from origin to cup, tracing the growers, traders, roasters, cafés, and communities that bring each bean across regions and continents before it reaches your hands. Together, we'll explore the networks, craftsmanship, and human stories that make coffee one of the world's most travelled beverages.Social Move invites guests to share stories from the road—memorable journeys, favourite commutes, unexpected detours, travel rituals, and the moments that altered our course. From everyday routes to life-changing adventures, we'll reflect on how movement often becomes the backdrop for our most meaningful memories.Hosted in an intimate setting designed for conversation and connection, the evening offers space to slow down, exchange stories, and appreciate the journeys—both ordinary and extraordinary—that bring us together.Summer Coffee Crawl 2025Stop 1 — Summer in the City14 June | Edit5, Sector 64, GurugramStop 2 — Coffee & Confessions21 June | Alayna Café, Shahpur JatStop 3 — Mode of Transport28 June | ATE Glasshouse, Lodhi RoadTickets- Single stop pass: Access to the event at that particular stop of the crawl- Three stop pass: Access to all 3 stops of the crawl

name: Soundgasms with Mohit - music production workshop
description: A 4-Week Music Production Workshop with Mohit Sen GuptaWhat does it take to turn an idea into a finished piece of music?Soundgasms with Mohit is a four-week deep dive into the art and craft of music production, led by producer, composer, and educator Mohit Sain Gupta. Designed for aspiring producers, musicians, artists, and curious beginners, this workshop focuses on the entire journey, from the first spark of an idea to a track that’s ready to be shared with the world.Across four weeks, Mohit will break down his creative process and introduce participants to the tools, techniques, and workflows used in contemporary music production. Through live demonstrations, hands-on exercises, and group feedback, you’ll learn how to build beats, shape sounds, arrange ideas, and bring your musical vision to life.What You’ll Learn•⁠ ⁠The fundamentals of music production•⁠ ⁠Beat-making and rhythm programming•⁠ ⁠Melody, harmony, and arrangement•⁠ ⁠Working with DAWs and production software•⁠ ⁠Sampling and sound selection•⁠ ⁠Recording and audio processing basics•⁠ ⁠Mixing fundamentals•⁠ ⁠Building an efficient creative workflow•⁠ ⁠Taking a track from concept to completionWho Is This For?•⁠ ⁠Aspiring music producers•⁠ ⁠Independent artists and musicians•⁠ ⁠Songwriters looking to self-produce•⁠ ⁠Creatives curious about electronic music and beat-making•⁠ ⁠Complete beginners looking for a place to startWorkshop Details•⁠ ⁠Duration: 4 Weeks•⁠ ⁠Format: In-person, interactive sessions•⁠ ⁠-Capacity: Limited to 20 participantsonly limited slots for participants to choose to attend only one session(2 classes)•-⁠ ⁠Led by: Mohit Sen GuptaWith only 20 seats available, Soundgasms is designed to be intimate, collaborative, and hands-on, giving every participant the opportunity to learn, experiment, and receive direct guidance throughout the program.Whether you’re opening production software for the first time or looking to refine your process, this workshop will help you understand not just how to make music, but how to develop a creative practice of your own.20 participants. 4 weeks. One journey from idea to finished sound.29th June - 22nd July, 2026, 4pm-6pm, every Monday and Wednesday. Venue: Baithak, Gully Labs store, Panchsheel Park

name: In Her Interest by Zerodha - Mutual Funds 101, Delhi
description: Women have a complicated relationship with money. And it starts early. Many families shield their daughters from anything related to ‘finance’, but this well-meaning protection ends up being a disservice. For when these young women step into the real world, they lack the confidence to make financial decisions - not because they’re incapable, but because they were never given information on budgeting, savings, and investments. Later in life, many continue to rely on their partners for investment decisions. This dependency on others will only stop when they have the right information and toolkit to take back control. That’s where we come in.We come back with our second 'In Her Interest'  session on 4th July in Delhi and will focus on one core idea:Mutual Funds 101 - all you need to know.﻿The session will be led by two amazing experts who truly get it.Shilpa Bhaskar GoleCertified Financial Planner® (CFP®) | Founder NerdyBird Wealth Advisory LLP - a Non-Individual SEBI Registered Investment Adviser (RIA)Neha SinghFounder at Womoneysta | Certified Financial Planner | SEBI-registered Once you buy a ticket, you also get to send a question that the speakers will address during the session.Note: Each session is theme-based. This session is about understanding Mutual Funds - we are not talking stocks and shares.
